name: next-js-patterns
description: Best practices and patterns for Next.js App Router, Server Actions, and Routing in this project.
capabilities: Guide on App Router page structure and component placement
Explain Server vs Client Component decisions
Show proper navigation patterns with Link and useRouter
Demonstrate data fetching in Server Components
Provide metadata and SEO configuration examples
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown documentation for Next.js patterns. No executable code, network calls, or file system access. The static analyzer flagged JSON string patterns as cryptographic/network threats, but these are metadata fields in a configuration file. This skill only provides guidance documentation.
valueStatement: Get best practices for Next.js 15 App Router including Server Components, layouts, and navigation patterns. Build modern React applications with proper architecture.
limitations: Contains project-specific patterns not generic Next.js documentation
Does not include code execution or file generation capabilities
Requires existing Next.js project to apply patterns

useCases.title: Learn App Router fundamentals
useCases.description: Understand page layouts, component types, and navigation in Next.js 15

useCases.title: Apply server data patterns
useCases.description: Fetch data in Server Components and handle client-side data needs

useCases.title: Structure Next.js projects
useCases.description: Design proper route layouts, metadata, and font optimization

promptTemplates.title: Basic page setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a new page with proper structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a new page in Next.js App Router with proper Server Component structure

promptTemplates.title: Client interaction
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a Client Component in Next.js App Router for state management

promptTemplates.title: Navigation flow
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the difference between Link and useRouter for navigation in Next.js

promptTemplates.title: Data fetching
promptTemplates.scenario: Fetching data from APIs or databases
promptTemplates.prompt: Show best practices for fetching data in Server Components versus Client Components

bestPractices: Default to Server Components for better performance and security
Use Link from next/link for all internal navigation links
Keep client-side logic in separate components with use client directive
Define metadata at page or layout level for SEO optimization
antiPatterns: Avoid using useRouter when Link component would suffice
Do not make all components Client Components unnecessarily
Avoid fetching data in Client Components when Server Components can handle it

faq.question: What versions of Next.js support App Router?
faq.answer: Next.js 13 and later versions support the App Router. This skill focuses on Next.js 15 patterns.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of nested routes supported?
faq.answer: Next.js supports deep nesting. Practical limits depend on project complexity and file organization.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with existing projects?
faq.answer: Apply patterns to new routes in app directory. App Router can coexist with pages directory.

faq.question: Does this skill access any external data?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides documentation. No data is read, written, or transmitted.

faq.question: Why is my page not updating with navigation?
faq.answer: Ensure you are using Link for navigation. Client Components using useRouter must be wrapped properly.

faq.question: How does this compare to the pages router?
faq.answer: App Router offers Server Components by default, nested layouts, and streaming. Pages router is still supported.
